title: PHP-FPM Max Children Reached Events
spl:
index=php sourcetype="phpfpm:status"
| eval mc=coalesce('max_children_reached', max_children_reached)
| where mc > 0
| sort - mc
| dedup host, pool
| table _time, host, pool, mc, active_processes, idle_processes
description: When `max children reached` increments, new requests queue at the socket and clients see 502/504 from nginx or Apache.
value: Direct signal to raise `pm.max_children` or fix slow PHP before outages.
implementation: Poll frequently enough to catch spikes; combine with listen queue metrics from UC-8.2.11 patterns.
visualization: Single value (pools in violation), table (pool detail), trend of `mc` delta.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Custom scripted input polling `pm.status_path`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=php` `sourcetype=phpfpm:status` (`max children reached` counter).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Normalize the counter name at HEC if spaces break extraction.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=php sourcetype="phpfpm:status"
| eval mc=coalesce('max_children_reached', max_children_reached)
| where mc > 0
| sort - mc
| dedup host, pool
| table _time, host, pool, mc, active_processes, idle_processes
```

Understanding this SPL

**PHP-FPM Max Children Reached Events** ‚Äî See the description and value fields in this use case JSON.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=php` `sourcetype=phpfpm:status` (`max children reached` counter). **App/TA**: Custom scripted input polling `pm.status_path`. Rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` to match your deployment.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scope to the documented index and sourcetype, then apply transforms, thresholds, and `timechart`/`stats` as in the SPL above.

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm events exist, field extractions match your format, and thresholds reflect production baselines.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add to a dashboard or alert; document ownership. Suggested visuals: Single value (pools in violation), table (pool detail), trend of `mc` delta..
grandmaExplanation: We catch when your PHP workers are completely used up so pages stop failing mysteriously.
